adds.rationale: Rocket Lab is the only publicly traded pure-play small-satellite launch company; its Q4 2021 earnings (released February 28, 2022) showed contracted backlog nearly tripling from $241M to $545M in a single quarter — the clearest commercial-milestone signal in the emerging launch economy and an early-buildup entry for the profile's rockets-and-spacecraft wave.

news_evidence.summary: Rocket Lab Q4 2021: full-year revenue $62.2M (+77% YoY), Q4 $27.5M above guidance, backlog surged $241M -> $545M as of Feb 28, 2022.
Rocket Lab launched 'The Owl's Night Continues' Electron mission Feb 28, 2022 from LC-1B for Synspective, demonstrating continued launch cadence.

adds.rationale: The Defiance Quantum ETF (QTUM, launched September 2018) is the most liquid pure-play quantum vehicle; IBM's November 2021 Eagle 127-qubit processor — the first to exceed 100 qubits and classical-simulation capacity — is the early-buildup hardware milestone the profile identifies for the quantum wave.

news_evidence.summary: IBM unveiled the Eagle 127-qubit processor on Nov 16, 2021 — the first to exceed 100 qubits, too large to reliably simulate classically.
IBM Eagle nearly doubles its 65-qubit predecessor and surpasses Google's 53-qubit Sycamore; roadmap shows 433-qubit Osprey (2022) and 1,121-qubit Condor (2023).

adds.rationale: Russia's Feb 24, 2022 invasion of Ukraine exposed Europe's dependence on Russian nuclear-fuel enrichment; uranium spot raced to a 10-year high as institutional buyers (Sprott bought 1.4M lbs on Feb 25 alone) front-ran supply disruption, making URA — the largest, most liquid uranium ETF — an early-buildup entry for the nuclear wave.

news_evidence.summary: Uranium spot surged to a 10-year high after the invasion; Sprott recorded its single largest day of uranium-trust purchases (1.4M lbs) on Feb 25, 2022.

Swedish utility Vattenfall announced Feb 24, 2022 it would halt planned Russian nuclear-fuel deliveries and place no new orders — a structural supply-chain shift supporting non-Russian uranium demand.

rationale_overall: This first quarterly rebalance fills three wave buckets the starter watchlist left uncovered. The AI-heavy core (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA) is sound (AAPL record Q1 revenue $123.9B, NVDA record Q4 FY2022 $7.64B), so no removes. The three adds each respond to a buildup-phase signal visible at March 31, 2022: RKLB's backlog tripling in a quarter; IBM Eagle's 127-qubit milestone putting quantum on institutional radar (QTUM); and the Ukraine invasion's immediate stress on Russian nuclear-fuel supply activating the nuclear energy-security thesis (URA). Defense (ITA) was passed over — defense equities had already surged 15-20% post-invasion, and the profile's rule is to enter on buildup, not chase a completed re-rating. Robotics (BOTZ) was deferred to a later rebalance as no comparable buildup catalyst was visible and three adds already fill the watchlist to the 8-cap.
